mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above trend signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above trend signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below trend signals a supply-driven contraction that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 7% signals a loosening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in loosening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above trend signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
